title: feat: journald log driver support for `--log-opt labels=` and `env=`
body:
Closes #28187

**- What I did**
Added support for `--log-opt labels=<comma-separated-labels>` and `--log-opt env=<comma-separated-env-vars>` when using the journald log driver. This promotes the specified container labels or environment variables as structured fields in journald, matching Docker's behavior.

**- How I did it**
In `pkg/specgenutil/specgen.go`, when iterating through `--log-opt` options, added cases for `labels` and `env`. These cases split the provided values by `,` and iterate through them. If the corresponding key is found in the container's labels (`s.Labels`) or environment (`s.Env`), the value is extracted and added to `logLabels`. The keys are automatically sanitized using `sanitizeLogKey` (replacing non-alphanumeric characters with `_` and converting to uppercase) to ensure compatibility with `journald` field name restrictions.

**- How to verify it**
```bash
podman run --log-driver=journald --log-opt labels=location --label location=west alpine echo test
journalctl -o json-pretty CONTAINER_NAME=...
# Should contain "LOCATION" : "west"
```

**- Description for the changelog**
Support for `--log-opt labels=` and `--log-opt env=` has been added to the journald log driver to promote container labels and environment variables as structured journal fields.
